bug_report.summary: Keyboard shortcuts table: “Shortcut” column header appearance/padding changed unexpectedly
steps_to_reproduce: Look at the table header row, specifically the “Shortcut” column header.
expected_behaviors: Table header styling (padding/alignment/label rendering) should remain consistent unless intentionally changed as part of the icon-column improvements.
actual_behaviors: The “Shortcut” header rendering differs after the change (bbox [780,188,851,205]), indicating an unintended adjustment to the header label’s padding/alignment/style compared to before.

bug_report.summary: Keyboard shortcuts filter view: Shortcut column header/value cell rendering changes (not limited to icon/action columns)
steps_to_reproduce: In the keyboard shortcuts search field, type: close.
Observe the “Shortcut” column header and the rendered shortcut-value cells (e.g., Ctrl+W, Escape, Ctrl+E).
expected_behaviors: UI changes should be confined to the narrow icon/action columns (alignment/spacing/consistency), as described in the change intent.
The “Shortcut” column header and shortcut-value cell styling (padding/alignment/text rendering) should remain unchanged unless explicitly intended.
actual_behaviors: Visual differences are detected in the “Shortcut” header and in multiple shortcut-value cells (diff regions: header bbox [780,188,851,205]; value cell bboxes [778,249,834,266], [779,307,833,327], [778,336,829,353]), indicating the change impacts non-icon columns as well.

bug_report.summary: Preferences > Export sort order: field selection dropdown arrow is rendered outside the field control (detached into separate narrow column)
steps_to_reproduce: In “Export sort order”, select “Use specified order”.
Look at the field selector in each row (e.g., ‘Citationkey (Internal)’, ‘Author’, ‘Title’).
expected_behaviors: The field selector is a single cohesive control (combo box) with its dropdown arrow integrated on the right edge of the field.
actual_behaviors: The field text box appears without an integrated dropdown arrow, while a separate narrow column shows standalone down-arrow buttons aligned in a separate strip, making the combo box look split/broken and potentially reducing clarity/click-target size.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Preferences > Export sort order: “+ Add” does not add a new sort criterion row (no visible change after click)
steps_to_reproduce: Click the “+ Add” button in the “Export sort order” area.
expected_behaviors: A new empty criterion row appears in the list/table, allowing the user to choose a field (e.g., author/year).
actual_behaviors: No new row appears; the table still shows the same three rows (Citationkey (Internal), Author, Title) after clicking “+ Add”.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: ‘Restart required’ dialog is visually corrupted after second Save (content shows underlying table / message missing)
steps_to_reproduce: Expand “File”, select “Close library”, press a new shortcut (e.g., Ctrl+Alt+J).
Click “Save”, then dismiss the ‘Restart required’ dialog.
Click the Reset icon for “Close library”.
expected_behaviors: A ‘Restart required’ dialog should consistently show the warning icon and text (‘Keyboard shortcuts changed… You must restart…’) with an OK button, as in the first Save.
actual_behaviors: On the second Save, the ‘Restart required’ dialog content area is incorrect: the warning/message content is missing and the dialog appears to show underlying Keyboard Shortcuts table rows (suggesting transparency or wrong content rendering).
